query: USMCA joint review July 1 2026 outcome - did US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? Latest news on Trump USMCA withdrawal or renewal decision June-July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is a summary of the key findings:

## Answer to the Research Question

**Did the US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? — NO.**

The July 1, 2026 outcome was a **non-renewal under Article 34.7** (the sunset/six-year review clause), which is legally distinct from — and less severe than — a formal withdrawal under Article 34.6.

### What actually happened:
- The US, Mexico, and Canada met virtually on July 1, 2026. USTR **Jamieson Greer** announced the US "did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form."
- This **triggered the annual joint review mechanism (Article 34.7.4)** — reviews now occur yearly instead of every 6 years, running until **July 1, 2036**.
- The **agreement remains fully in force** with unchanged market access; it was **not terminated**.
- Both **Canada** and **Mexico** wanted the full 16-year renewal (which would have run to 2042); only the US declined.

### The critical Article 34.6 distinction:
Canadian law firm **Cassels LLP** stated most explicitly: *"The US has **not formally withdrawn** from CUSMA as any Party is permitted to do under **Article 34.6** by providing written notice of withdrawal... The US decision not to renew triggers **Article 34.7(4)**."*

### Trajectory / open possibility:
- USTR Greer told Politico that Trump *could* still decide to withdraw ("revise it, review it or exit it"), but **no Article 34.6 withdrawal notice has been executed**.
- A 16-year extension remains available at any time before 2036 if all three heads of government confirm in writing.
- The US prefers bilateral talks; a third round with Mexico was set for the week of July 20, 2026, while a formal process with Canada had not yet launched.

Structured findings (key facts, sources, and quantitative data points) have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Ambassador Greer Issues Statement on the USMCA Joint Review
sources.snippet: The United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form. As a result, the USMCA is not renewed. However, the Agreement remains in force pending resolution of these issues or until the Agreement's termination.

sources.title: CUSMA Still in Force Notwithstanding US Decision to Not Renew
sources.snippet: The US has not formally withdrawn from CUSMA as any Party is permitted to do under Article 34.6 by providing written notice of withdrawal to the other Parties. The US decision not to renew triggers Article 34.7(4) which mandates an annual joint review until July 1, 2036.

sources.title: USMCA 2026 Joint Review: United States declines to extend Agreement, triggering annual reviews
sources.snippet: United States declines to extend Agreement, triggering annual reviews (published 2026-07-02).

sources.title: U.S. Declines to Renew the USMCA/T-MEC/CUSMA, Setting Stage for Annual Review Cycles
sources.snippet: The US decision does not terminate the Agreement. Rather, it triggers the Agreement's annual review mechanism, under which the parties will meet each year to determine whether the Agreement should be extended before its scheduled expiration in July 2036.

sources.title: U.S. Declines to Renew CUSMA at First Joint Review: What Businesses Need to Know
sources.snippet: The non-renewal triggers an annual joint review process under Article 34.7.4 of CUSMA, which will recur each year until the parties either agree to an extension or it expires in 2036.

sources.title: Trump Administration Decides Not To Extend USMCA for Another Term: What Does That Mean Exactly?
sources.snippet: Article 34.7 text and analysis: a failure to confirm the wish to extend at the 6-year mark triggers annual reviews; a decision to extend can be made at any time before 2036.

sources.title: Trump Official Warns President May Leave His Own Signature Trade Deal
sources.snippet: USTR Greer said Trump could decide to withdraw from USMCA: 'The reason why we built a review period into USMCA was in case we needed to revise it, review it or exit it.'

sources.title: Trump Administration Decides Against Renewing USMCA, Opts for Annual Review Process
sources.snippet: On July 1, the US formally declined to renew USMCA, triggering an annual review process that extends negotiations for up to a decade.

sources.title: United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement
sources.snippet: On July 1, 2026, the US announced it would not renew the agreement. The signatories will now engage in an annual review for the next 10 years until the agreement expires on July 1, 2036 or one gives 6 months notice of withdrawal.

sources.title: U.S. won't renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations with Canada and Mexico
sources.snippet: The Trump administration decided not to renew its trilateral trade pact, instead opting to conduct annual reviews.

key_facts: On July 1, 2026, the US declined to renew/extend USMCA for a new 16-year term during the first mandatory joint review.
The US did NOT provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6; the action was a non-renewal under Article 34.7, which is legally distinct from withdrawal.
USTR Jamieson Greer stated: 'The United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form. As a result, the USMCA is not renewed. However, the Agreement remains in force.'
The non-renewal triggered the annual joint review mechanism under Article 34.7(4), which now recurs each year until July 2036.
The USMCA/CUSMA remains fully in force until at least July 1, 2036, with unchanged preferential market access.
Both Canada (Minister Dominic LeBlanc) and Mexico (President Sheinbaum via Secretary Marcelo Ebrard) formally supported the 16-year renewal, which would have extended the pact to 2042.
A 16-year extension remains available at any time before 2036 if all three heads of government confirm in writing.
Cassels LLP explicitly confirmed the US has NOT formally withdrawn under Article 34.6 (which requires written notice); the non-renewal only triggered Article 34.7(4) annual reviews.
The US prefers bilateral over trilateral negotiations; a third round of bilateral talks with Mexico was scheduled for the week of July 20, 2026.
A formal joint review/negotiation process with Canada had not yet been launched as of early-mid July 2026; US-Canada relations described as more strained.
USTR Greer told Politico that Trump could still decide to withdraw from USMCA, but no Article 34.6 withdrawal notice has been executed.
Article 34.6 withdrawal requires 6 months' written notice to the other parties; where a party withdraws, the agreement remains in force for the remaining parties.

data_points.metric: US Article 34.6 written withdrawal notices provided as of July 13 2026
Scheduled USMCA expiration date under sunset clause (absent extension)
Annual joint review frequency after non-renewal (per Article 34.7.4)
Length of new term if renewal had been agreed
Withdrawal notice period required under Article 34.6
Scheduled third round of US-Mexico bilateral negotiations
